title: Stapelia ciliolata
intro: Stapelia ciliolata, also known as the 'Carrot Stapelia', is a succulent plant species native to Southern Africa, characterized by its striking appearance and unique, star-shaped flowers. The Stapelia ciliolata plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stapelia ciliolata include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stapelia ciliolata is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Stapelia ciliolata is classified in the kingdom Plantae, in the order Apiales, and in the family Apocynaceae. The genus Stapelia belongs to the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. It is further classified into the order Asparagales, which is an outdated classification but is sometimes used. However, in modern classifications, Stapelia ciliolata is typically placed in the order Asparagales and family Apocynaceae. It is also known to be native to South Africa and Namibia.
